source.title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with suspected or diagnosed asthma, excluding severe asthma and acute attacks.
source.setting: Primary and specialist diagnosis, monitoring, and chronic asthma management.
source.exclusions: Exact for chronic asthma assessment and monitoring; excludes severe asthma and acute attacks and is not directly validated for UAE pathways.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

items.rationale: Supports mapped wheeze identity, duration, episodic pattern, triggers, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, inhaler and bronchodilator-response context, nocturnal symptoms, respiratory examination, peak flow, presets, and history.

items.final_wording: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

items.rationale: Supports mapped severe-breathlessness, chest-symptom, stridor, respiratory-distress, cyanosis, and safety fields as prompts only.

items.final_wording: Supports peak flow, pulmonary function testing, spirometry, bronchodilator reversibility, and selected clinician-led investigations.

source.title: Asthma: diagnosis, monitoring and chronic asthma management
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with suspected or diagnosed asthma, excluding severe asthma and acute attacks.
source.setting: Primary and specialist diagnosis, monitoring, and chronic asthma management.
source.exclusions: Exact for chronic asthma assessment and monitoring; excludes severe asthma and acute attacks and is not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.6 — clinical history, examination, objective tests, and occupational asthma

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, symptom variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, atopy and family history, occupational context, respiratory examination, peak flow, FeNO, spirometry, and objective confirmation.

added_items.rationale: Corroborates mapped wheeze, symptom, examination, peak-flow, and spirometry fields; no duplicate item is mapped.

added_items.final_wording: Supports review of symptoms, time off work or school, reliever use, systemic steroid courses, admissions or emergency attendance, validated control questionnaires, peak flow, FeNO, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking or vaping, occupational, psychosocial, seasonal and environmental factors, and response to treatment change.
